rationale: The citing article is an editorial content-coding review of 401 JMR papers (2013–2019), and the cited 2016 JMR conjoint paper appears to fit as one unit in that corpus rather than as a conceptual or methodological source. In the provided notes, prior-work engagement is with broad historical-review papers (e.g., Huber et al. 2014), with no specific discussion of this cited paper’s findings, and the match evidence is only a bare DOI reference entry. This looks like an incidental/listing citation in a journal audit context.
